expectations.text: AI surfaces the S3-success / DB-write-failure partial failure scenario
expectations.evidence: Baseline provided a clean happy-path implementation flow. Step 4 says 'S3 stores the file', Step 5 says 'backend saves the S3 file URL' with no mention of what happens when these two steps are not atomic.

expectations.text: AI asks about who can access whose files
expectations.evidence: Baseline used user-scoped S3 keys (e.g., 'profile-pictures/{userId}/...') as a pattern, but never asked who should be able to access which files or raised presigned vs public URL access control as a decision to make.

expectations.text: AI asks about file size and type limits
expectations.evidence: Baseline included file type and size validation in the happy-path flow: 'checks auth, validates category and MIME type' — though this was presented as implementation detail, not a probing question to the user.

expectations.text: Generated prompt includes edge cases with the partial failure scenario as a concrete decision
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated. The baseline produced a how-to implementation guide with no structured edge case decisions.

expectations.text: Generated prompt defines compartmentalized components with individual responsibilities
expectations.evidence: No structured handoff prompt generated. Components were not explicitly defined with single responsibilities and interface contracts.
